sanskrit: य एनं वेत्ति हन्तारं यश्चैनं मन्यते हतम् । उभौ तौ न विजानीतो नायं हन्ति न हन्यते ॥
hindiTranslation: जो मनुष्य इस अविनाशी शरीरीको मारनेवाला मानता है और जो मनुष्य इसको मरा मानता है, वे दोनों ही इसको नहीं जानते क्योंकि यह न मारता है और न मारा जाता है ॥
englishTranslation: Whoever thinks this imperishable being is the killer, and whoever thinks it is killed, both do not understand. It neither kills nor is killed.
essence: What you are cannot be reduced to killer or victim.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: The true self cannot be killed, and it does not kill. Seeing it as either one is ignorance.
simpleInsight: Fear loosens when you stop identifying life with what can be destroyed.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna stands frozen before battle. Krishna has just said the body ends, but the inner being does not. Now he sharpens the point: neither murder nor death touches that reality, so Arjuna’s terror is built on a false identification.
modernRelevance: You are about to send the message that ends a friendship, or walk away from a role that once defined you. The mind says, 'I am ruining everything.' This verse says the deepest part of you is untouched by such labels.
translationLiteral: यः (who) / एनम् (this) / वेत्ति (knows) / हन्तारम् (as killer) / यः (and who) / च (also) / एनम् (this) / मन्यते (thinks) / हतम् (killed) / उभौ (both) / तौ (those two) / न (not) / विजानीतः (understand) / न (not) / अयम् (this) / हन्ति (kills) / न (not) / हन्यते (is killed)
insight: It shifts the mind from personal guilt and fear to clear seeing of what can and cannot be harmed.

quoteVariants.text: You are not the killer, and you are not the killed.
I am not the one who kills, nor the one killed.
